title.zh: 基山上九尾四耳、目生于背的怪羊
title.en: Nine-Tailed, Four-Eared Goat-Beast with Eyes on Its Back

summary.zh: 基山异兽，形状像羊，长九条尾巴、四只耳朵，眼睛长在背上；把它的皮毛佩在身上，人就不知恐惧。
summary.en: A beast of Mount Ji shaped like a goat, with nine tails, four ears, and eyes set on its back; wearing its hide was said to banish fear.

description.zh:
《南山经》记基山"有兽焉，其状如羊，九尾四耳，其目在背，其名曰猼訑，佩之不畏"。基山山南多玉，山北多怪木，山上还住着一种三首六目、六足三翼的怪鸟。猼訑的怪处全在器官的错位与冗余：羊的身子上叠出九条尾巴、四只耳朵，一双眼睛不长在脸上，偏偏开在脊背。

"佩之不畏"是它存在的理由——把它的皮毛佩在身上，人就胆气充盈、无所畏惧。《山海经》的功效体系里，有治聋的旋龟、防迷惑的灌灌、宜子孙的鹿蜀，猼訑分管的则是恐惧。一头浑身是眼耳、时刻警觉的兽，皮毛却能让人心安，这条巫术逻辑颇为直白：兽替人长出了多余的耳目，佩者便不必再怕背后的暗处。明清图本多把它画成温顺的长毛羊，背上一对圆眼，是图卷里最惹人端详的形象之一。
description.en:
Mount Ji, jade-bearing on its southern slope and covered in strange trees to the north, is home to the boshi. The Classic of the Southern Mountains describes a beast shaped like a goat but with nine tails and four ears, its eyes set upon its back. Every organ is either doubled or displaced: the face is blind, while the spine keeps watch.

Its reason for being lies in the closing formula, 'wear it and you will not fear'. In the book's economy of virtues — where the xuangui turtle guards hearing, the guanguan bird dispels confusion, and the lushu blesses descendants — the boshi holds the portfolio of courage. The magical logic is plain enough: a creature bristling with extra ears and back-mounted eyes is alertness incarnate, and whoever carries its hide borrows that vigilance, no longer fearing what stands behind him. Ming and Qing illustrated editions tend to draw it as a placid, shaggy goat with a pair of round eyes staring up from its back, one of the most quietly unsettling figures in the picture scrolls.

value.en: Nine tails, four ears, eyes on its back
